mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 1.5% quarterly signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 1.5% coincides with negative 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the consumer price index exceeding 0.4% month-over-month forces markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Markets absorb CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% without negative 5-day returns, yielding a positive Sharpe ratio on the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.0% annualized indicates economic stagnation that suppresses corporate revenue forecasts, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth below 1.0% fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with signal accuracy dropping below 48% in late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production reflect immediate improvements in factory output and supply chain efficiency, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly increases show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.
